rust_basics.no_std development: Use #![no_std] at crate root. No standard library available. Use core:: instead of std:: for basics (core::str, core::fmt, core::cell, core::arch). Use alloc crate for heap allocation: extern crate alloc; use alloc::vec::Vec; use alloc::string::String;
rust_basics.no_main in firmware: Use #![no_main] to prevent Rust from expecting a standard main function. Define custom entry point via #[entry] attribute from the uefi crate. Signature: fn main() -> Status { ... }.
rust_basics.panic = abort: In Cargo.toml: [profile.dev] panic = "abort" and [profile.release] panic = "abort". This prevents unwind table generation, reducing binary size and fixing linker errors in no_std.
rust_basics.UnsafeCell pattern: Used for interior mutability in static variables. Wrap your type: pub struct SafeType { inner: UnsafeCell<Type> }. unsafe impl Sync for SafeType {}. Methods access via unsafe { &mut *self.inner.get() } or unsafe { &*self.inner.get() }.
rust_basics.Global allocator: Use #[global_allocator] static ALLOCATOR: AllocatorType = AllocatorType::new(). The uefi crate provides one with features = ["alloc", "global_allocator"]. For custom: implement GlobalAlloc trait.
rust_basics.Static initialization: In no_std, statics must be const-initializable. Use const fn new() for constructors. Collections like Vec::new() are const. Wrap in Option and call init() at runtime if needed.
rust_basics.UEFI crate key types: Status (return from entry), Handle (protocol handle), ScopedProtocol (safe protocol wrapper), SystemTable (access to all UEFI services), BootServices (memory, protocols, events), RuntimeServices (time, vars, reset).

rust_compiler_errors.E0432: unresolved import: The imported item doesn't exist. Check the path: use uefi::proto::media::fs::SimpleFileSystem (not uefi::proto::console::file::SimpleFileSystem). Check crate version features.
rust_compiler_errors.E0308: mismatched types: Expected type A, got type B. Common in no_std when using std types instead of core types. Use core::slice::from_raw_parts instead of std::slice::from_raw_parts.
rust_compiler_errors.E0133: use of unsafe block: Operation requires unsafe {}. Dereferencing raw pointers, calling unsafe functions, accessing mutable statics all need unsafe blocks.
rust_compiler_errors.E0277: trait bounds not satisfied: Type doesn't implement a required trait. E.g., writing to stdout needs write_str from core::fmt::Write. Add use core::fmt::Write; for write! macro.
rust_compiler_errors.E0463: can't find crate: Dependency not in Cargo.toml or not downloaded. Run cargo fetch or cargo build. For target-specific crates, ensure --target is correct.
rust_compiler_errors.E0512: incompatible ABIs: Mismatch between C ABI expectations. Common when mixing UEFI types with standard Rust types. Use repr(C) or repr(packed) on structs passed to firmware.
rust_compiler_errors.Warnings: unused variables: Prefix unused variables with underscore: _var instead of var. Or use #[allow(unused_variables)] at function level.
rust_compiler_errors.Warnings: dead code: Code never called. Use #[allow(dead_code)] or prefix with underscore. In OS dev, some functions are intentionally unreachable from user code.

rust_unsafe_patterns.Raw pointer dereference: let ptr: *mut T = ...; unsafe { *ptr = value; }. Must ensure pointer is aligned, non-null, and points to valid memory. Prever wrap in safe API.
rust_unsafe_patterns.Volatile access: In embedded/OS, use write_volatile and read_volatile to prevent compiler optimizations from removing hardware register accesses. Example: ptr.write_volatile(value) or ptr.read_volatile().
rust_unsafe_patterns.Inline assembly: Use core::arch::asm! macro. Syntax: asm!("instructions", out("reg") out_val, in("reg") in_val, options(nomem, nostack)). Common in PCI I/O (in/out instructions) and context switching.
rust_unsafe_patterns.Union types: Use core::mem::MaybeUninit<T> for potentially uninitialized memory. .assume_init() to read as initialized. Must guarantee initialization before reading.
rust_unsafe_patterns.Transmute: use core::mem::transmute. Only when types have same size. Extremely dangerous. Prever safer conversions like bytemuck crate or manual field-by-field copy.

cargo_commands.Build with warnings: cargo build 2>&1 | head -30 (see only first 30 lines)
cargo_commands.Check (fast): cargo check --target x86_64-unknown-uefi (no codegen, just typecheck)
cargo_commands.Test (host): cargo test --target x86_64-unknown-uefi (limited, most tests need host target)
